attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- commit-optimizer: Create structured optimization commit messages with measured performance/size results. Use this whenever you're committing code optimizations (especially RISC-V libm assembly tuning) and want to automatically benchmark the changes and generate a properly formatted commit message with problem, approach, and result sections. Invoke with `/commit`.
- libm-testpoints: IEEE 754 numerical analysis expert for generating representative libm test points. Use this skill whenever the user asks to generate test points, test cases, or test inputs for math library (libm) functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.), whether float or double precision. Also trigger when the user wants to improve, audit, or extend existing test_*.c files in the RISC-V libm test harness. Trigger on requests like "generate 100 test points for sinf", "what are the key test cases for expf?", or "add representative tests to test_logf.c". The argument is the function name, e.g. "/libm-testpoints sinf" or "/libm-testpoints sin".
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions on AX45MPV (VLEN=1024). Runs a 7-step FSM — select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, profile, learn — then loops back to analyze with new pipeline insights. No human intervention required.
- wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /optimize learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session

content.content:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 2ce599fe..b561e11d 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -150,17 +150,12 @@ Vector internal headers are versioned: `internal_vec_isa_v0_8.h`, `internal_vec_
 - **Public API** (`Include/`): `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_activation.h`, `riscv_nn_types.h`, etc.
 - **Internal** (`internal/`): `internal_nn_math.h` (math macros/inline helpers), `internal_dsp_isa.h` (DSP intrinsics), `internal_vec_*.h` (vector intrinsics)
 
-### CMSIS-NN Compatibility
-
-`cmsis_include/` provides adapter headers (`convert_between_andes_nn_and_cmsis_nn.h`) mapping ARM CMSIS-NN APIs to libnn equivalents. Supports CMSIS-NN 3.0, 3.2, and 4.1.
 
 ## Commit Message Convention
 
 Prefix with category tag in brackets:
 - `[opt/rvv]` — RISC-V vector extension optimization
-- `[opt/dsp]` — DSP extension optimization
 - `[bug-fix]` — Bug fix
-- `[new fun]` — New function added
 - `[fine-tune]` — Algorithm tuning
 - `[misc]` — Maintenance/cleanup
 
diff --git a/docs/wiki/index.md b/docs/wiki/index.md
index 0c73f00c..ab9af240 100644
--- a/docs/wiki/index.md
+++ b/docs/wiki/index.md
@@ -46,9 +46,13 @@ Reference for the `/optimize` skill. Read this file first in `select` to identif
 | [[conv-hwc-s8-asym-bias-any]] | 4.97x | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] + [[im2col-strategies]] | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
 | [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] | 2.60x | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]] + [[runtime-dispatch]] | Optimized 2026-03-25 |
 | [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]] | 2.57x | Direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
+| [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] | 2.31x (cifar10) | VQMACC direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
+| [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.09x conv2, 1.05x conv3 (cifar10) | 4-pixel batch [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
 | [[avepool-hwc-s8]] | 2.78x | [[e16-accumulation]] | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
 | [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] | 1.30x | [[im2col-strategies]] pad=0 | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
 | [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] | ~1.0x | Memory-bound -- no further opportunity | Exhausted 2026-03-23 |
+| [[fc-mat-vec-s16-s16-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | ~1.0x (gru) | Weight layout locks algorithm; N-vectorize blocked | Algorithm exhausted 2026-04-14 |
+| [[fc-s8-s8-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.107x (kws-dnn), 1.16x IP2/IP3 | 2-row-group outer batching (shared input load) | Round 1 improved 2026-04-14 |
 
 ---
 
@@ -58,8 +62,9 @@ Reference for the `/optimize` skill. Read this file first in `select` to identif
 |---|---|
 | `conv_HWC_*_sym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], then operator page |
 | `conv_HWC_*_asym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], [[im2col-strategies]], operator page |
+| `conv_HWC_*_sft_*` | [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] (4-pixel batch with unroll4) |
 | `conv_1x1_*` | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]], [[runtime-dispatch]], [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] |
-| `conv_HWC_*_RGB_*` | [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]] |
+| `conv_HWC_*_RGB_*` | [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] |
 | `avepool_*` | [[e16-accumulation]], [[avepool-hwc-s8]] |
 | `maxpool_*` | [[memory-bound-diagnosis]], [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] |
 | Any conv with dilation | [[im2col-strategies]] (pad=0 path), [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] |
diff --git a/qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh b/qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh
index a041aa70..1d6a6974 100755
--- a/qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh
+++ b/qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@
 #
 # Environment:
 #   MYPC   — FPGA board host (default: sw-boards.andestech.com)
-#   MYPORT — FPGA board port (default: 1116)
+#   MYPORT — FPGA board port (default: 1115)
 ################################################################################
 set -uo pipefail
 
@@ -47,7 +47,7 @@ WRAPPER="${BOARD_DIR}/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
 
 # FPGA board connection
 export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
-export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"
+export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1115}"
 
 # Validate
 for f in "${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/riscv64-elf-gcc" "${TEST_SRC}" "${WRAPPER}"; do
diff --git a/test_perf.sh b/test_perf.sh
index 111d3b2f..03db9468 100755
--- a/test_perf.sh
+++ b/test_perf.sh
@@ -56,7 +56,7 @@ if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
     export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
 fi
 export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
-export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"
+export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1115}"
 
 CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
 CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case

The untracked list mixes source/docs with build artifacts (ELFs, `build/`, `build_perf_*/`, `test_sh_*/`, `.claude/worktrees/`, `project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done`, etc.). I'll commit source + docs only and skip artifacts. Confirm before I proceed?

Planned to commit:
- Modified: `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/wiki/index.md`, `qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh`, `test_perf.sh`
- New docs: `docs/wiki/operators/{conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast,conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast,fc-mat-vec-s16-s16-s8-sft-bias-fast,fc-s8-s8-s8-sft-bias-fast}.md`
- New examples: `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/{cifar10,gru}/{Makefile,baseline.md}`, `gru/nds_ops_stub.c`, `kws_dnn/{Makefile,dnn_weights.h,KWS-DNN-baseline.md,nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c}`

Skipping: `*.elf`, `hello.c`, `test_rvv.c`, `build/`, `build_perf_*/`, `test_sh_*/`, `perf_comparison_*.txt`, `.claude/worktrees/`, `project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done`, `qemu-validate/results/`, `release/`.

Proceed?
